name: Tandori-Style Game Hens with Corn and Cumin-Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 each Cornish game hens, 1.5 pounds total
3 1/2 cups plum tomatoes, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
FOR HENS: Combine the buttermilk, cilantro, garlic, ginger, serrano pepper, and turmeric in a medium bowl. Place the game hens in a 15x10x2-inch glass baking dish. Pour the mixture over the hens. Cover and chill overnight.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Transfer the chilled hens to a heavy 17x11x1-inch baking pan. Sprinkle both sides of the hens with salt and pepper. Pour the marinade (buttermilk mixture) over the hens. Bake, basting occasionally with juices, until cooked through, about 1 hour and 10 minutes.
MEANWHILE, PREPARE THE SAUCE: Heat oil in a heavy medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onions and sauté until golden, about 6 minutes. Add tomatoes, wine, and cumin; sauté until tomatoes are tender, about 10 minutes. Puree the mixture in a blender and return to the saucepan. Stir in cream and simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Keep warm.
FOR CORN: Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add corn, cumin, and turmeric; sauté until corn is heated through, about 5 minutes. Stir in cilantro. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon some sauce onto the center of each plate. Place the cooked hens atop the sauce. Spoon corn onto plates, dividing equally.
